name: cursor-toys
description: Supercharge your Cursor AI workflow! Share commands instantly, test APIs in-editor, manage personal libraries, and boost team collaboration. The ultimate productivity toolkit for Cursor users.

explorer/context.when: resourcePath =~ /[\\/]\.(cursor|claude|vscode|ai|cursortoys)[\\/](commands|rules|prompts|notepads|plans|skills|http|kanban)/ || resourcePath =~ /[\\/]\.(cursor|vscode|ai|cursortoys)$/ || resourceFilename == hooks.json || resourceFilename == SKILL.md

enumDescriptions: Use a custom base URL (configure in cursorToys.customBaseUrl)

cursorToys.customBaseUrl.description: Custom base URL for deeplinks (only used when linkType is 'custom'). Example: https://example.com/link/ or custom://protocol/

cursorToys.allowedExtensions.description: Allowed file extensions (without the dot, e.g., md, mdc, txt, json). For rules, .mdc is recommended to support metadata.

cursorToys.baseFolder.description: Base folder name for storing commands, rules, prompts and HTTP files (e.g., 'cursor', 'vscode', 'ai'). Note: Rules and prompts are Cursor-specific features.

cursorToys.extensionDataFolder.description: Folder name for CursorToys-exclusive data (Kanban, Notepads). Stored as .{name}/ in your home directory and workspace (default: .cursortoys/).

cursorToys.commandsFolder.description: Folder to save commands: 'cursor' for .cursor/commands or 'claude' for .claude/commands. Only the commands folder is configurable; rules and prompts always use .cursor

enumDescriptions: Show commands from both .cursor and .claude folders
cursorToys.personalCommandsView.description: Which command folders to show in Personal Commands view: 'both' for .cursor and .claude, 'cursor' for .cursor only, or 'claude' for .claude only

cursorToys.codeAnchors.enabled.description: Enable code anchors (bookmarks on specific lines with gutter icons, sidebar list, and navigation).

cursorToys.codeAnchors.showStatusBar.description: Show forward/backward navigation controls in the status bar when anchors exist in the workspace.

cursorToys.sidebar.hiddenViews.markdownDescription: Explorer sidebar sections to hide under CursorToys. Keys: `notepads`, `kanban`, `clipboard`, `commands`, `prompts`, `plans`, `skills`, `hooks`, `mcpb`, `http`, `projects`. Empty list shows all sections. Command Palette and file actions still work for hidden resources.

cursorToys.sidebar.explorerViews.markdownDescription: Explorer sidebar sections to also show in the Explorer (in addition to the CursorToys activity bar). Keys: `notepads`, `kanban`, `clipboard`, `commands`, `prompts`, `plans`, `skills`, `hooks`, `mcpb`, `http`, `projects`. Default: `skills`, `plans`.

cursorToys.httpRequestTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP requests (default: 10 seconds)

cursorToys.httpRequestSaveFile.description: Also write the HTTP response to a .res/.response file on disk. The response is always shown in the response panel (or editor, if configured).

enumDescriptions: Show responses in a reusable webview panel (updates in place on re-run)
Open each response in the text editor (legacy behavior; may open many tabs)
cursorToys.httpRequestResponseView.description: Where to display HTTP responses after Send Request.

cursorToys.httpRequestEditor.enabled.description: Open .req/.request files from the HTTP folder in the visual request editor (Postman-style) instead of plain text.

cursorToys.httpRequestEditor.autoSave.description: Automatically save changes from the HTTP request editor while you edit.

cursorToys.httpAssertionsEnabled.description: Enable HTTP request assertions validation (@assert annotations)

cursorToys.httpAssertionsShowInline.description: Show assertion results inline in .res files

cursorToys.httpAssertionsFailOnError.description: Stop execution if assertion fails (extension only)

cursorToys.httpDefaultEnvironment.description: Default HTTP environment name (used for reference in status bar)

cursorToys.clipboard.enabled.description: Enable in-editor clipboard history (ring buffer on copy/cut).

cursorToys.clipboard.bindStandardKeys.description: When true, Ctrl+C / Ctrl+X in the editor are routed through clipboard history (disable if another extension conflicts).

cursorToys.clipboard.maxEntries.description: Maximum number of entries kept in clipboard history.

cursorToys.clipboard.maxEntryChars.description: Maximum characters per history entry; larger copies are skipped.

cursorToys.clipboard.syncWithSystem.description: When pasting from history or snippet slots, also write the text to the system clipboard.

cursorToys.clipboard.previewChars.description: Characters shown in the clipboard history Quick Pick preview.

cursorToys.minify.preserveComments.description: Preserve comments when minifying code (currently not implemented)

enumDescriptions: Ask each time before creating a gist
cursorToys.gistDefaultVisibility.description: Default visibility when creating GitHub Gists

cursorToys.recommendationsEnabled.description: Enable CursorToys recommendations based on project context

cursorToys.recommendationsCheckOnStartup.description: Check for recommendations when opening a workspace

cursorToys.skillsRegistryUrl.description: URL to Agent Skills registry from Tech Leads Club. Contains community-driven skills catalog for AI coding assistants.

cursorToys.geminiModel.description: Gemini AI model to use for text refinement

cursorToys.geminiRefinePrompt.default:
You must return ONLY the refined text, nothing else. Do not add introductions like "Here's the refined version" or "Okay, here is". Do not add markdown separators (---). Do not add explanations or notes. Just return the improved text directly.

Fix typos, improve clarity, and enhance the flow of the following text:
cursorToys.geminiRefinePrompt.description: Default prompt template for AI text refinement

cursorToys.geminiRequestTimeout.description: Timeout in seconds for Gemini API requests (default: 30)

cursorToys.mcp.enabled.description: Enable the built-in CursorToys MCP server (stdio). Requires extension host for workspace access.

cursorToys.mcp.autoRegister.description: Automatically add/remove the cursor-toys entry in ~/.cursor/mcp.json when MCP is enabled/disabled.

cursorToys.mcp.port.description: Reserved for optional HTTP MCP transport (0 = auto). Stdio transport uses IPC bridge.

cursorToys.mcp.allowDestructiveWithoutCo.description: When true, MCP delete/clear tools do not require confirm: true (not recommended).

cursorToys.mcp.disableClipboardViaMcp.description: When true, clipboard history MCP tools are disabled.

cursorToys.mcp.auditLogEnabled.description: Write MCP tool invocations to .cursortoys/mcp-audit.log in the workspace.

cursorToys.mcpb.useOfficialCli.description: Use the official MCPB CLI (npx @anthropic-ai/mcpb) for verify and unpack when available. Enables signature verification for signed packages and uses the same unpack logic as the official tooling. Falls back to built-in extraction if the CLI is not available.

cursorToys.cli.cursortoysPackageSpec.markdownDescription: npm package spec for **CursorToys CLI** when the extension runs `npx cursortoys …` (HTTP tests, etc.). Examples: `@latest`, `2026.5.30`, `^2026.5.0`.

cursorToys.spending.enabled.description: Show Cursor API usage (Auto % and API %) in the status bar. Set to false to hide the spending indicator.

cursorToys.spending.autoDetectToken.description: Build the session cookie from Cursor's local login (state.vscdb). If false or detection fails, the manual session token is used.

cursorToys.spending.sessionToken.description: Optional override: full WorkosCursorSessionToken cookie value or JWT from cursor.com. Used when auto-detect is disabled or fails.

cursorToys.spending.refreshInterval.description: Refresh interval for spending usage in minutes.

cursorToys.usageMonitor.refreshIntervalM.description: Refresh interval for OpenRouter / DeepInfra status bar items (minutes).

cursorToys.usageMonitor.openRouter.showI.description: Show OpenRouter remaining credits in the status bar. Set API key via Usage Monitor in CursorToys Settings.

cursorToys.usageMonitor.deepInfra.showIn.description: Show DeepInfra account balance in the status bar. Set API key via Usage Monitor in CursorToys Settings.

cursorToys.kanban.showStatusBar.description: Show a Kanban icon in the status bar for quick access to the Kanban board.

cursorToys.notepads.showStatusBar.description: Show a Notepads icon in the status bar to focus the Notepads sidebar view.

cursorToys.projects.enabled.description: Enable the Projects sidebar and dashboard to pin, categorize, and reopen frequent workspaces.

cursorToys.projects.recentLimit.description: Maximum number of workspaces kept in the Recent list.

cursorToys.projects.openInNewWindow.description: Open projects in a new window instead of replacing the current window.

cursorToys.projects.openDashboardOnStart.description: Open the Projects dashboard when Cursor starts (requires projects.enabled).

scripts.test:http: npm run compile && node out/httpDynamicVariables.test.js && node out/httpRequestExtensions.test.js && node out/httpRequestVariables.test.js && node out/httpVariableResolver.test.js && node out/httpRequestParser.test.js
scripts.test:usage-monitor: npm run compile && node out/providerUsage/openRouterCredits.test.js && node out/providerUsage/openRouterKeyInfo.test.js && node out/providerUsage/openRouterActivity.test.js && node out/providerUsage/usageMonitorPanelState.test.js && node out/providerUsage/deepInfraBilling.test.js
scripts.test:mcp: npm run compile && node out/mcp/security.test.js && node out/mcp/commandRegistry.test.js && node out/mcp/mcpWave4.test.js
scripts.test:projects: npm run compile && node out/projects/projectRegistry.test.js && node out/projects/projectPathUtils.test.js
